channel.title: Night Owl
channel.description: Automatic update feed for Night Owl.

item.title: Version 1.7 (build 20)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.7 (build 20)

  • Fixes target-catalog sync between your Macs. A long-standing conflict in how catalog and progress records were stored meant that once a target had been imaged and verified, its catalog details (name, type, coordinates, goal) could never sync between machines — so the same object could look different on each Mac. Those details now converge correctly across all your Macs and devices. Update every Mac to this build.

item.title: Version 1.7 (build 19)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.7 (build 19)

  • More reliable target-catalog sync across your machines. A Mac that can’t reach iCloud no longer republishes over the shared catalog, and an auto-created placeholder can never overwrite a target that’s already matched to a real object — so your targets stay consistent on every Mac and device instead of a stub clobbering the good data. Ungrouping a mosaic, or removing a panel from one, now propagates everywhere too.
  • Mosaic tracking reads correctly. Each panel and the project header now measure against the mosaic’s per-panel goal (not each panel’s own goal or the summed total), so a mosaic’s progress and “weakest panel” completion line up.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 17)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 17)

  • Group a multi-panel mosaic into one tracked project. A mosaic shot as several Seestar panels — each plate-solving to a different object — now folds into a single row in the Tracker with combined hours and a “weakest panel” progress bar (a mosaic counts as complete only when every panel reaches its goal). Group from the suggestion banner when Night Owl spots same-night neighboring panels, or select targets and choose “Group as Mosaic…” — and ungroup anytime. Grouped panels archive together under the project folder, and grouping syncs across your Macs.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 16)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 16)

  • Add, edit, and delete tracker targets from any machine. The catalog is now shared and multi-writer — a target you add or edit on the Workstation (or the iPhone/iPad app) syncs to the Observatory and back, and deletes propagate everywhere. Edits use last-write-wins, and captured hours are preserved across edits.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 15)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 15)

  • Fixes a crash when using the menu bar on macOS 26. An informational item in the Review menu could crash the app while the menu was being built. If Night Owl was quitting unexpectedly around menu use, this resolves it.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 14)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 14)

  • Ideal time of year for every target — the target detail popup now shows the best season to image each object from your site: a peak month, a "now" line, and a 12-month strip banded into prime and usable months, with a live in-season / shoulder / out-of-season badge and the current month marked. All computed from the target's coordinates — nothing to enter.
  • Roomier detail popup — the target popup is now a two-column layout (reference image, description and capture history on the left; tonight's visibility and the season strip on the right), so it fits without scrolling.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 13)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 13)

  • Cull results reach the Calibration card reliably — after you verify a night on the Workstation, the observatory now picks up that keep-quality within about a minute and updates “Calibration,” instead of leaving the night stuck on “cull pending” until the app was relaunched.

item.title: Version 1.6 (build 12)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6 (build 12)

  • Archived nights leave “On Drive” on their own — once the Workstation archives a night to the SSD, the pipeline now clears it from “On Drive” to “Archived” within a minute or two, without waiting for the observatory to capture again. Fixes counts that stayed stuck at “On Drive” for nights you’d already processed.

item.title: Version 1.6
item.description:
Night Owl 1.6

  • Sessions and Pipeline are now one panel — the Sessions list is grouped by where each capture is in its journey (On Seestar → Uploading → On Drive → Archived). Your own sessions stay fully editable; captures on the other machine appear as read-only status rows, so each Mac sees the whole pipeline. With nothing selected, the detail pane shows the pipeline overview.
  • Hour-by-hour forecast for the night — the Tonight card now shows an hourly strip and a headline like “Usable imaging ≈ 1.5h · roof likely opens ~4:00am,” so a night that only clears at 4am reads very differently from one that’s open all night.
  • Smarter, honest predictions — image-quality learning now comes only from your verified culls on the Workstation (not the automatic dawn pass), so the model tunes to what you actually keep. Your “fully labelled” count may drop as it switches to verified nights.
  • Stuck uploads self-heal — a session left part-uploaded by a Drive hiccup is now retried automatically.

item.title: Version 1.5 (build 10)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.5 (build 10)

  • Pipeline in the sidebar — the unified Pipeline tab introduced in build 9 is now actually reachable: it was missing from the sidebar. It appears on every node, under Sessions.
  • One pipeline, everywhere — the observatory now publishes the complete session lifecycle (On Seestar → Uploading → On Drive → Archived) as a single shared view, so the Workstation — and soon the iPhone/iPad app — show exactly the same list, including session sizes.

item.title: Version 1.5 (build 9)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.5 (build 9)

  • “Cull” is now “Sessions” — and it's available on the Observatory too, so you can see each night's captures on the machine that records them.
  • Unified Pipeline view — one Pipeline tab on every node shows where each night's capture is on its way from the scope to the archive: On Seestar → Uploading → On Drive → Archived, with counts and progress. Captures still on the Seestar that haven't been uploaded to Google Drive are now clearly visible, and the upload status flows through so you can see it from the Workstation as well.

item.title: Version 1.5
item.description:
Night Owl 1.5

Power-loss resilience — for when the scope loses power overnight.

  • Battery safety cutoff — if the Seestar drops to a set charge while off wall power, Night Owl automatically stops the plan and parks the scope to protect it, then holds until it's back on power. Set the floor in Settings ▸ Observatory ▸ Battery safety cutoff (default 10%).
  • Shore-power alert — you're notified the moment the scope switches from wall power to battery, giving hours of warning before it runs low.
  • Capture recovery — captures left on the scope when it lost power are pulled automatically once it's back online (and there's a “Pull captures from Seestar now” button to do it on demand).
  • Companion updates — the phone now receives power-lost, battery-stop, and recovery alerts, shows whether the scope is on wall power, and can park the scope, pull captures, or adjust the battery floor remotely.

item.title: Version 1.4
item.description:
Night Owl 1.4

  • Pipeline tab on the Workstation — a new rail tab that shows your staged capture nights moving from Queued to Archived, with counts, how much is still waiting to transfer, and how long the oldest upload has been queued. It reads the same records your iPhone and iPad do, including nights already archived and cleared off the Drive.
  • Capture status in the Tracker — each night in a target's breakdown now carries a Queued or Archived tag, so you can see a night's pipeline state without leaving the target.
  • More accurate cross-machine crediting — hardened how a capture night is keyed so the Observatory and Workstation always agree on which night a session belongs to, keeping per-night integration totals correct across both Macs.

item.title: Version 1.3
item.description:
Night Owl 1.3

  • Reimagined Targets tab — a sky workspace with three views you switch between: Tonight (an altitude-vs-time chart of your targets with twilight and Moon), Sky radar (where each target sits in the sky right now), and Atlas (the photographic star map). A target list on the right stays in sync across all three.
  • Cleaner sky map — the Atlas view drops the old web-style controls for native ones, including zoom in/out.
  • Two devices at once — pair both your iPhone and iPad; each is now tracked independently, so they no longer interfere when sending commands. Manage and revoke paired devices in Settings ▸ Companion.
  • Session pipeline on your phone & iPad — staged sessions now show their progress from Queued to Archived. Choose how long to keep archived records in Settings ▸ Cloud.
  • Fixes — remove a target folder from the Process tab; clearer errors when a processing step fails; smoother scrolling on the Conditions tab; captures logged under an alias now credit the right target.

item.title: Version 1.2
item.description:
Night Owl 1.2

  • Remove a target folder from Process — right-click a target in the Process list and choose “Remove Target Folder…” to drop it (with confirmation). Your archived files on disk are never touched.
  • Clearer processing errors — when a Siril step fails, the Process tab now shows the actual error inline (with a “Reveal log in Finder” button) and writes a full per-step log, instead of a bare red ✗.
  • Smoother Conditions tab — the radar & satellite maps no longer re-load their tiles on every update, so scrolling to that row is no longer laggy.

item.title: Version 1.1 (build 3)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.1 (build 3)

  • AI processing suggestions — the Process tab has a new “Suggest settings” button that asks Claude to recommend stacking, drizzle, gradient/color, stretch, and BlurX/NoiseX/StarX settings tuned to a target's integration time and scope. Add your own Anthropic API key in Settings ▸ AI (stored only in your Keychain).
  • Fixes a first-run processing failure — the integration step could fail the first time it ran because its output folder hadn't been created yet. It's now created up front, with a clear error if that ever fails.
  • Sky map search & add-from-sky — search or go to any object on the sky map, and add a target directly from where the map is centered.
  • Live sky conditions — the Conditions tab now shows live weather plus aurora/geomagnetic (Kp) activity and a dew-point margin, with an optional alert when a strong geomagnetic storm is forecast.
  • Cleaner Tracker — removed the “verified hours” line that wasn't reporting reliably; per-night integration totals are unchanged.

item.title: Version 1.1
item.description:
Night Owl 1.1

  • Reclaims disk space on the Workstation — after a night's verified keepers are archived to your external drive, the local working copies are removed automatically, so the processing folder no longer grows without bound.
  • Process from your archive — on the Workstation, the Process tab now works from archived target folders (Add Target Folder…), with integration hours read from the folder itself rather than imported sessions whose files have been reclaimed.
  • Settings tailored to each node — the Settings panel now hides options that don't apply to the selected node type.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
Night Owl 1.0

First release distributed with built-in automatic updates.

  • In-app updates: Night Owl now checks for new versions and updates itself.
  • Accurate capture hours: integration time is reconciled per observation night, so a target's total reflects every night it was imaged.
  • Sky map, plate-solve pointing checks, and mosaic planning.
